conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you the person responsible for Alex? That is, are you the one who is mainly responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. I'm the one who looks after Alex day-to-day - I take care of all their needs and they live here with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Do you normally live in the UK, and are you either a UK citizen, or do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through settled status, pre-settled status, or a visa that gives you the right to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

You have:
- A child (Alex) who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- You normally live in the UK and are a UK citizen

Before I provide my final assessment, I need to confirm one more thing: Is Alex under 16 years old, or if they're 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is only 8 years old, so definitely under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- You are a UK citizen and normally live in the UK

You should be able to apply for Child Benefit successfully. Please note that if you or your partner has an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay a tax charge (the High Income Child Benefit Charge), but you would still be eligible to claim the benefit itself.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 years old. The claimant is responsible for Alex and Alex lives with them. The claimant normally lives in the UK and is a UK citizen. The claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care. All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met: the child is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for the child, and the claimant has the right to claim Child Benefit as a UK citizen living in the UK.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, they are the person responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, and they are a UK citizen normally living in the UK. There are no factors that would make them ineligible.
